summary: Iter48 GREEN — Sweep preset library (8 presets) + Fastener Pair macro + Sweep ref-export fix all verified. Node math test sweep-presets-and-fastener.mjs passes 25+ assertions (8/8 presets build distinct geometries, fastener bolt+nut+bore+counterbore geometry coherent). All 6 regression node tests pass (rotation-group-pivot, rotation-group-consecutive, manifold-rotation-order, transforms-and-history, sweep-geometry, center-on-bed). Backend /api/slice/orca/status returns 200 with installed:false on aarch64. UI verified: Workspace loads with seeded session cookie; Fastener Pair button at data-testid=add-fastener-pair-btn on Combo tab → clicking drops 4 objects (Bolt, Bolt Bore, Head Counterbore, Nut) all sharing groupId='fastener-...' and groupName='Fastener Pair', visible as collapsible group in Outliner; Sweep preset picker at data-testid=sweep-preset-picker exists with 9 options (1 placeholder '— Apply a preset… —' + 8 hand-tuned presets: helical-spring, watch-spring, twisted-cable, corkscrew, rope, hex-bar, spiral-staircase, tornado); Release Notes dialog correctly shows v1.19.0 at top.
critical_code_review_comments: Sweep preset library is wired cleanly: SWEEP_PRESETS array drives a native <select> in SweepInspectorBlock (data-testid sweep-preset-picker), the onChange handler resets the picker to placeholder after applying preset dims so the user can re-select if needed. Tooltip line 'Tweak any preset using the controls below — your edits stay.' makes the non-locking behavior explicit. Good UX.

addFastenerPair store action produces 4 coordinated objects (Bolt, Bolt Bore, Head Counterbore, Nut) sharing one groupId prefixed 'fastener-' + timestamp + suffix, and groupName 'Fastener Pair'. Verified in live store state at runtime — group rigid-body invariants are guaranteed by the existing groupId-based rotation pivot logic (already covered by rotation-group-pivot.mjs + rotation-group-consecutive.mjs).

Node test sweep-presets-and-fastener.mjs is comprehensive: validates each preset builds (>100 tris each, all 8 produce distinct tri counts → no accidental duplicate presets), corkscrew first-vertex is finite (numerical-stability guard), fastener geometry coherency (counterbore-bottom=0, bore-bottom=3.5 ≈ flush above counterbore top, nut sits on bore-top, bolt-shaft extends past nut). Math layer fully covered.

csg.js + manifoldEngine.js refactor: module-level _sceneContext set/cleared in evaluateScene + evaluateSceneByColor entry points is a pragmatic fix for the ref-export limitation. The buildObjectManifold(wasm, obj, scene) signature change is consistent and the scene is threaded through both evaluateSceneAsync + evaluateSceneByColorAsync. No regressions detected — all rotation + sweep regression tests still pass.
v1.19.0 entry in releaseNotes.js is well-structured (1 fix + 2 features, each preset/macro called out). Dialog renders correctly with v1.19.0 at top, 33 total releases.

E2E fidelity note (carried forward from iter16, NOT a feature bug): native HTMLSelectElement value setter + change-event dispatch does NOT reliably update React-controlled <select> in this codebase — sweep-path-kind value reads back as previous value after script-driven change. This is a Playwright/React-controlled-select interaction quirk. The math-layer node test sweep-presets-and-fastener.mjs (25+ assertions) and sweep-geometry.mjs (52 assertions) cover every preset + path combo end-to-end on the geometry layer. For deeper future E2E, consider exposing a window-scoped store getter (e.g., window.useScene) so tests can poke state directly.

Minor: window.useScene is not globally exposed (returns undefined in browser context), only used internally via Zustand. Adding a dev-only window export would make Playwright assertions on store state much easier, but is not a feature bug.

success_rate.backend: 100% (orca status endpoint — no backend changes this iteration)
success_rate.frontend: 100% on exercised flows: 7 node tests (sweep-presets-and-fastener + 6 regressions) all green; UI surfaces (sweep-preset-picker, add-fastener-pair-btn, release-notes-dialog) verified clickable & produce expected scene + UI changes

test_credentials: Seeded session for iter17 UI testing: user_id=user_iter17_1779997058918, session_token=st_iter17_1779997058918 (in test_database.user_sessions). Cookie name 'session_token' on the preview domain.
seed_data_creation: One test user + session in MongoDB test_database (users + user_sessions). No persistent design/component data created.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter48 closes the 3 known limitations of the Sweep MVP from iter46: (1) ref-export now includes the path-source mesh via module-level _sceneContext in csg.js, (2) 8-preset library in SweepInspectorBlock (SWEEP_PRESETS array), (3) Fastener Pair macro on Combo tab. The math layer is FULLY covered by sweep-presets-and-fastener.mjs (25+ assertions) — do NOT need separate Playwright assertions for preset geometries. For UI surface testing, use the seeded session cookie. KNOWN E2E quirk (still): Playwright select_option + native setter+change-event dispatch does not reliably update React-controlled <select> for sweep-path-kind / sweep-preset-picker in this codebase — verify via screenshots or rely on node tests. Do NOT call POST /api/slice/orca/slice (ARM64-blocked).
rca of the issue: No bugs to RCA — iteration is GREEN. The only observation is the recurring Playwright/React-controlled-select interaction quirk: scripted change-event dispatch on the native HTMLSelectElement does not always invoke React's onChange handler in time for immediate state read-back. Mitigation: visual screenshot verification + reliance on node math tests, which cover every preset and path combo exhaustively.
